---
title: "Application I: Vine Copulas for Multivariate Dependence"
---

::: {.callout-note}
Slides for this session will be posted shortly before the course.
:::

**Speaker:** Judith Claassen | Vrije Universiteit Amsterdam

::: {.columns .align-items-center}
::: {.column width="20%"}
![](../../images/judith_github.jpg){.rounded-circle width="130px"}
:::
::: {.column width="80%"}
Judith Claassen is a researcher at the Institute for Environmental Studies (IVM) at Vrije Universiteit Amsterdam. She earned her bachelor degree in Earth and Environmental Sciences at the Amsterdam University College, and holds a master in Civil Engineering: Water Management from TU Delft. Her research focuses on modelling multi-hazard events using probabilistic methods, such as vine copulas. Her PhD was funded by the Horizon 2020 MYRIAD-EU project, where she also served as the first chair of the Early Career Researcher board
:::
:::

## Overview

This presentation introduces vine copulas as a flexible framework for modelling
multivariate dependence structures in compound events. You will work through
two hands-on notebooks:

- **Part 1** covers bivariate copulas: copula families, the probability integral
  transform, fitting, CDF/PDF computation, and conditional/unconditional sampling.
- **Part 2** extends to vine copulas: C-vines and D-vines for modelling
  higher-dimensional dependence structures relevant to compound hazard analysis.

The [VineCopulas](https://github.com/VU-IVM/VineCopulas) Python package (v2.0.3)
is used throughout. It is developed by VU-IVM and freely available on GitHub.

## Setup

You need **Python 3.10+** and the `vinecopulas` package. Install it with pip:

```bash
pip install vinecopulas jupyterlab "pandas<2.2" "numpy<1.27" "matplotlib<3.9"
```

Then launch Jupyter and open the notebook files:

```bash
jupyter notebook
```

## Notebooks

::: {.schedule-table}

| Notebook | Content | Download |
|---|---|---|
| Part 1: Bivariate Copulas | Copula families, probability integral transform, fitting, CDF/PDF, conditional sampling | [bivariatecopulas.ipynb](bivariatecopulas.ipynb) |
| Part 2: Vine Copulas | C-vines, D-vines, multivariate dependence, simulation | [vinecopulas.ipynb](vinecopulas.ipynb) |

:::

## Slides

*Slides will be posted shortly before the course.*
